lede: A look at August 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Alfred Hitchcock and Fidel Castro, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Democratic Republic of the Congo
events.desc: One hundred fifty-six Congolese Tutsi refugees are massacred at the Gatumba refugee camp in Burundi.

events.title: Central African Republic
events.desc: The Central African Republic declares independence from France.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The German Luftwaffe launches an air operation, codenamed Adlertag, during the Battle of Britain in an attempt to destroy the British Royal Air Force.

events.title: 25th Infantry Regiment (United States)
events.desc: The all black infantrymen of the U.S. Army's 25th Infantry Regiment are accused of killing a white bartender and wounding a white police officer in Brownsville, Texas, despite exculpatory evidence; all are later dishonorably discharged. (Their records were later restored to reflect honorable discharges but there were no financial settlements.)

events.title: 1868 Arica earthquake
events.desc: The 8.5–9.0 Mw Arica earthquake struck southern Peru with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme), causing 25,000+ deaths and a destructive basin wide tsunami that affected Hawaii and New Zealand.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: Johann Sebastian Bach leads the first performance of Nimm von uns, Herr, du treuer Gott, BWV 101, a chorale cantata on a famous tune.

events.title: War of the Spanish Succession
events.desc: War of the Spanish Succession: Battle of Blenheim: English and Imperial forces are victorious over French and Bavarian troops.

events.title: George Monck, 1st Duke of Albemarle
events.desc: Colonel George Monck of the English Army forms Monck's Regiment of Foot, which will later become the Coldstream Guards.

events.title: Maurice (emperor)
events.desc: Maurice becomes Emperor of the Byzantine Empire.

events.title: Augustus
events.desc: Octavian holds the first of three consecutive triumphs in Rome to celebrate the victory over the Dalmatian tribes.

births.name: Fidel Castro
births.line: Cuban lawyer and politician, ex-President of Cuba

births.name: Karl Liebknecht
births.line: German politician, co-founded Communist Party of Germany

births.name: Richard Willstätter
births.line: German-Swiss chemist and academic, Nobel Prize Laureate

deaths.text: H. G. Wells, English novelist, historian, and critic
Eduard Buchner, German chemist, Nobel Prize laureate
Emperor Wen of Sui, emperor of the Sui Dynasty

observances.text: Christian feast day: Florence Nightingale, Octavia Hill (Lutheran Church)
